Name: Parada Równości (Warsaw Pride)
Description: Warsaw’s annual Equality Parade and city-centre Pride programme. The 2026 edition used Equality Town in Park Świętokrzyski and a central march; follow the organiser for each year’s route, programme and afterparties.
Opening_Notes: Citywide Pride programme. Check the official site for the next edition.

Name: Instytut Bar
Native_Map_Query: Instytut Bar, ul. Prosta 2/14, Warsaw, Poland
Description: A central gay cruise bar and adult club with a cocktail bar, sauna facilities and a large darkroom. Its themed programme includes nude, sportswear and fetish-oriented nights, so check the current event post before heading over.
Vibe_Tags: Bar, Cruising, Darkroom, Men Only, Cocktails, Fetish/Gear
Opening_Notes: Check the current Instagram programme for opening nights, themed events and door rules.

Name: Sauna The Fire
Native_Map_Query: Sauna The Fire, Twarda 44, Warsaw, Poland
Description: Large modern gay sauna in central Warsaw. The current guide lists regular themed promotions and naked-party programming; check the venue’s current schedule before going.
Opening_Notes: Check the current venue programme for themed nights and entry conditions.

Name: HEAVEN Sauna
Description: Gay sauna listed in Warsaw’s current nightlife guide, with regular themed days including naked and bi-focused sessions. Check the current venue programme for the address, opening pattern and event details.
Opening_Notes: Confirm the current address and themed-night schedule directly before travelling.

Name: Galla Klub Sauna
Description: Long-running Warsaw gay sauna retained in current 2026 travel coverage. A sensible option to keep on your shortlist if you want a traditional sauna rather than a club night.
Opening_Notes: Check current opening times and access rules before travelling.

Name: Sauna Stare Miasto
Description: Gay sauna listed in current Warsaw sauna coverage. Keep it as a practical back-up when the larger venues are busy, but verify the latest opening details before setting off.
Opening_Notes: Check the current listing or venue channels for hours and entry details.

Name: D-Men Sauna Club
Description: Gay sauna club included in current Warsaw sauna coverage. Check the latest listing directly for opening details before you go.
Opening_Notes: Check current hours and access information before travelling.

Name: reFForm Fetish Shop
Native_Map_Query: reFForm Fetish Shop, ul. Prosta 2/14, Warsaw, Poland
Description: Fetish and gear shop in the same central building as Instytut Bar. Useful for picking up kit before an event, especially on a themed night.
Opening_Notes: Check current shop hours before visiting.

Name: Poznań Pride
Description: Poznań’s citywide Pride season, organised with the Stonewall Group. It combines the Equality March with cultural, social and party programming across June.
Opening_Notes: Citywide programme. Check the official schedule for future dates and venues.

Name: HaH Club
Description: Heaven and Hell is a well-known Poznań LGBTQ+ club space with dance-floor nights and an art-led identity. Check its current programme before travelling because sessions and door policy can vary.
Opening_Notes: Check the current club calendar and venue details before attending.

Name: Amigo Sauna Club
Native_Map_Query: Amigo Sauna Club, Osiedle Lecha 120, Poznań, Poland
Description: Established Poznań gay sauna with a long local history. A more low-key option than the big city club scenes, suited to a sauna stop rather than a late dance night.
Opening_Notes: Check current opening times directly before travelling.

Name: Lokum Stonewall
Description: LGBTQ+ friendly bar in Poznań and a practical social start point before the city’s later club options. It is especially useful for visitors who want a relaxed, clearly queer-friendly first drink.
Opening_Notes: Check current social channels for opening hours and programme.

Name: WROPRIDE / Wrocław Equality March
Description: Wrocław’s annual Pride programme and Equality March, run with Kultura Równości. The programme includes wider city events alongside the march, so this works as the parent listing for planning a Pride-weekend visit.
Opening_Notes: Citywide programme. Check Kultura Równości for the next festival schedule.

Name: Cactus Club
Native_Map_Query: Cactus Club, Aleksandra Zelwerowicza 18A, Wrocław, Poland
Description: Wrocław’s main gay cruise club, with a bar, dance area, cinema and darkrooms across a large venue. It regularly runs naked and underwear-themed nights, making it one of the city’s most direct options for a sex-positive night out.
Vibe_Tags: Bar, Cruising, Darkroom, Naked, Underwear, Dancefloor, Smoking Area, Fetish/Gear
Opening_Notes: Usually closes around 04:00 on Thursday/Sunday and 06:00 on Friday/Saturday; check current theme and door policy.

Name: Surowiec
Description: Queer club-gallery and bar at Ruska 46a, with cocktails, art events, live performance, drag and club nights. Good if you want a more mixed queer crowd than the cruise-focused options.
Vibe_Tags: Bar, Dancefloor, Cocktails, Queer, Drag, Social
Opening_Notes: Closing time runs later from Thursday to Saturday; check the current programme.

Name: Tri-City Equality March
Description: Annual Gdańsk Pride march and wider Tri-City LGBTQ+ programme, organised with the local community. The festival is useful if you are visiting Gdańsk, Sopot or Gdynia around early summer.
Opening_Notes: Citywide Pride programme. Check Tolerado for the next route and companion events.

Name: Pub Red Light
Description: Alternative Gdańsk pub with a clearly LGBTQ+-friendly reputation and a relaxed social feel. It is a better fit for drinks and conversation than a dedicated gay club night.
Opening_Notes: Check current social channels for opening hours and events.

Name: Copenhagen Pride
Description: Copenhagen’s annual Pride festival, centred on a citywide programme, Pride Square activity and the parade. It is one of the easiest Nordic Pride trips to build a weekend around because most nightlife is concentrated around the centre.
Opening_Notes: Citywide festival. Check the official site for the next programme, Pride Square and parade details.

Name: Oscar Bar & Cafe
Native_Map_Query: Oscar Bar & Cafe, Regnbuepladsen 9, Copenhagen, Denmark
Description: Classic LGBTQ+ café-bar on Regnbuepladsen with a large terrace, food, drinks and Saturday DJs. It is one of the easiest places to start an evening because it opens long before the late bars.
Opening_Notes: Usually later on Friday and Saturday, with a Saturday DJ from around 22:00.

Name: Centralhjørnet
Description: Historic gay pub in the centre, with a traditional low-key bar feel rather than a polished nightclub atmosphere. Good for a proper pub drink and an older local crowd.
Opening_Notes: Friday and Saturday normally run until 04:00.

Name: Centre Stage
Description: LGBTQ+ bar with karaoke, drag, performances and a later-night atmosphere. This is the one to pick when you want the bar night itself to have a bit of show and camp.
Opening_Notes: Friday and Saturday usually run until 05:00.

Name: Jailhouse CPH
Description: LGBTQ-oriented basement bar in a playful jailhouse theme. Despite the name it is a social drinking bar, not a cruise venue.
Opening_Notes: Friday and Saturday usually run until 05:00.

Name: Masken Bar
Description: One of Copenhagen’s long-running gay bars, still a dependable part of the Studiestræde strip. It is a straightforward place to drink, meet people and move on later.
Opening_Notes: Friday and Saturday open earlier and usually run until 05:00.

Name: Men's Bar
Description: Small local gay bar near the centre, known for a regular crowd and a bear-friendly, mature feel. Better for a quiet drink and flirting than a full club night.
Opening_Notes: Check current hours before visiting.

Name: Never Mind Night Club
Native_Map_Query: Never Mind Night Club, Axeltorv 3, Copenhagen, Denmark
Description: Late LGBTQ+-friendly club by Axeltorv with DJs, a dancefloor and a smoking room. A reliable choice when you want to move on from the bars without leaving the centre.
Opening_Notes: Friday and Saturday normally run until 07:00.

Name: RUST Natklub
Description: LGBTQ+-friendly dance club in Nørrebro. It is not a dedicated gay venue, but is useful for a queer-friendly late club option outside the compact city-centre bar strip.
Opening_Notes: Check the current programme and door policy.

Name: SLM Copenhagen
Description: Members-only gay fetish club with two bars, play space, wet room, maze and smoking lounge. Dress code matters here, so do not turn up in ordinary club clothes without checking the event rules.
Vibe_Tags: Bar, Cruising, Darkroom, Men Only, Dresscode, Smoking Area, Fetish/Gear
Opening_Notes: Usually Friday and Saturday plus selected Thursdays/Sundays. Membership and theme-specific dress code are required.

Name: Body Bio
Description: Long-running central cruising venue with a discreet basement entrance. It is more adult club than sauna, with a mixed but mostly male crowd and a lower-key setup than SLM.
Opening_Notes: Usually closes around 23:00–00:00; check current hours before going.

Name: Amigo Sauna
Description: Large gay sauna across three floors, with sauna facilities, cabins, a darkroom and a maze. It is central and practical for visitors, sitting right beside the city’s main gay-bar area.
Opening_Notes: Tuesday–Thursday and Sunday usually until 04:00; Friday/Saturday usually until 07:00. Mondays are normally closed.

Name: Homoware
Description: Gay sex and fetish shop in the middle of Studiestræde, selling gear, underwear and toys. Convenient when you need kit before a party or club event.
Opening_Notes: Opening time and closing time vary by day; check before travelling.

Name: Stockholm Pride
Description: Annual Stockholm Pride festival with Pride House, Pride Park, a city parade and a large club programme. The official 2026 schedule ran 27 July–1 August, so this parent listing is here for future festival planning.
Opening_Notes: Citywide festival. Check the official programme for the next edition.

Name: Bar Replik
Description: Newer LGBTQ+-friendly café-bar on Södermalm, opened in 2025, with wine, cocktails, DJs and live music. A good early evening stop before the city’s clubs.
Opening_Notes: Check current social channels for exact daily hours.

Name: House of Q
Native_Map_Query: House of Q, Malmvägen 1, Stockholm, Sweden
Description: Queer community restaurant, café, workspace and event venue that turns into a party, performance and cultural space at night. This is the useful choice when you want something more social and mixed than a gay bar.
Opening_Notes: Check the current House of Q programme for opening hours and evening events.

Name: Side Track
Native_Map_Query: Side Track, Wollmar Yxkullsgatan 7, Stockholm, Sweden
Description: Gay bar and small restaurant on Södermalm with a bear-friendly crowd. One of the more relaxed, dependable places for food, drinks and conversation before a later night.
Opening_Notes: Kitchen usually runs until 23:00.

Name: The Secret Garden
Native_Map_Query: The Secret Garden, Kornhamnstorg 59, Stockholm, Sweden
Description: Old Town restaurant, lounge and wine bar with a mixed queer-friendly crowd. It is more of a flexible drinks-and-dancing option than a dedicated gay bar.
Opening_Notes: Check the current programme for DJ and event nights.

Name: Backdoor
Description: Large LGBTQ+ nightclub south of the centre, with a proper dancefloor and late weekend hours. Worth the trip when you want a full club night rather than bar-hopping in Södermalm.
Opening_Notes: Check the current event programme and door policy.

Name: Patricia
Native_Map_Query: Patricia, Söder Mälarstrand, Kajplats 19, Stockholm, Sweden
Description: LGBTQ+-friendly Sunday club night on the MS Patricia, a moored ship with several bars and dancefloors. Good as a Sunday option when much of the city has gone quiet.
Opening_Notes: Door entry is usually until 03:00.

Name: SLM Stockholm
Native_Map_Query: SLM Stockholm, Wollmar Yxkullsgatan 18, Stockholm, Sweden
Description: Men-only members’ leather and fetish club in Södermalm, with strict event dress codes and cruising. This is for properly gear-focused nights rather than casual drop-in clubbing.
Vibe_Tags: Bar, Cruising, Darkroom, Men Only, Dresscode, Fetish/Gear
Opening_Notes: Private club: membership, event rules and dress code apply. Check the current calendar.

Name: Blue Vision
Description: Large gay cruise and fetish club with cabins, darkroom, cinema, private booths and a sex shop. It also runs naked, underwear, jockstrap and mixed nights, so check the current event format before visiting.
Vibe_Tags: Cinema, Cruising, Darkroom, Naked, Underwear, Fetish/Gear
Opening_Notes: Check current theme nights and entry rules.

Name: Manhattan Biograf
Description: Gay cruise cinema with video booths, darkroom, cabins and other adult facilities. More direct and no-frills than the fetish-club model, with daily daytime-to-midnight opening.
Opening_Notes: Closed on Christmas Eve; check current event notices.

Name: Torso
Native_Map_Query: Torso, Galleria Passagen, Kornhamnstorg 49, Stockholm, Sweden
Description: Gay-oriented shop for underwear, beachwear and accessories, with brands such as ES Collection, Addicted and Andrew Christian. Handy for clothes and gear rather than a nightlife destination on its own.
Opening_Notes: Often later in summer; check before visiting.

Name: Chokladkoppen
Description: Gay-friendly Old Town café on Stortorget, popular for a daytime coffee, breakfast or low-pressure meet-up. Useful between sightseeing and the later venues rather than a night-out destination.
Opening_Notes: Winter opening starts later; check current hours.

Name: Mälarpaviljongen
Description: Seasonal waterfront café, beer garden and lounge bar on Kungsholmen with a relaxed LGBTQ+-friendly atmosphere. Best used as a sunny afternoon or early-evening stop in warm weather.
Opening_Notes: Generally April–September and weather-dependent.

Name: Helsinki Pride
Description: Finland’s biggest Pride week, with a citywide cultural programme, parade and free park festival. It is a proper week-long visit rather than just one march, and the main events are normally in late June.
Opening_Notes: Citywide programme. Check the official Pride site for the next edition.

Name: Street Pride Bar
Native_Map_Query: Street Pride Bar, Malminrinne 1, Helsinki, Finland
Description: Campy queer dive bar in Kamppi with a lounge, DIY karaoke and themed nights. This is the clearly queer bar option when you want a casual drink with a bit of silliness.
Opening_Notes: Check current social channels for opening hours and karaoke nights.

Name: Hercules
Description: Long-running Helsinki gay nightclub with a dancefloor, drag and a mainly over-30 crowd. Use it for the late club part of the night rather than a quiet drink.
Opening_Notes: Check current programme and opening times directly before attending.

Name: Mann's Street
Description: Gay karaoke bar near the central nightlife area, with a mixed-age but particularly middle-aged gay crowd. It is a proper local oddball rather than a polished cocktail place.
Opening_Notes: Check current hours before visiting.

Name: Room Bulevard
Description: Modern gay-friendly restaurant and bar with wine, beer and a courtyard terrace in summer. Better for a date, dinner or an early drink than for a heavy club night.
Opening_Notes: Check current restaurant and bar hours before visiting.

Name: Pub Fairytale
Description: Small artsy gay bar in Kallio with a terrace, craft beer and occasional karaoke. A useful alternative to the central venues when you want something more local and low-key.
Opening_Notes: Check current opening hours and events before visiting.

Name: Sauna Con Hombres
Native_Map_Query: Sauna Con Hombres, Kruunuvuorenkatu 3, Helsinki, Finland
Description: Finland’s dedicated gay sauna, now operating as Sauna Con Hombres. It has Finnish and steam sauna facilities, a bar, video room and darkroom, and works best as an early evening-to-late-night stop.
Opening_Notes: Wednesday, Thursday and Sunday normally until 22:00; Friday/Saturday normally until midnight.

Name: Keltainen Ruusu (Yellow Rose)
Native_Map_Query: Keltainen Ruusu (Yellow Rose), Malminrinne 2-4, Helsinki, Finland
Description: Major adult shop and gay meeting point in Kamppi, with a large cruising area, private rooms, slings, cinema and lounge space. It is both a shopping stop and a practical adult venue.
Opening_Notes: Check current shop and cruising-area hours before visiting.

Name: US Video Mega Sex Store
Native_Map_Query: US Video Mega Sex Store, Helsinki
Description: Adult shop and cruising venue near Kamppi, with private rooms, slings and a common lounge. It is one of the city’s regular adult-space options rather than a conventional bar.
Opening_Notes: Check current opening hours and entry details before going.

Name: MSC Finland – Tom's Club
Description: Men’s fetish and leather club operating as a members’ community and event organiser. It is useful as a searchable parent listing for special nights rather than a daily drop-in venue.
Vibe_Tags: Party, Cruising, Men Only, Dresscode, Fetish/Gear
Opening_Notes: Members’ events and venue details vary. Check the current organiser calendar before attending.

Name: X-Bar
Description: Long-running gay bar with a dance hall and quieter seating areas. It is the obvious first stop for an uncomplicated gay night in Tallinn.
Opening_Notes: Check current social channels for opening hours and event nights.

Name: Sauna Club 69
Native_Map_Query: Sauna Club 69, Sakala tn 22a, Tallinn, Estonia
Description: Men-focused gay sauna and club with Finnish and aroma saunas, pool, bar, video room and private cabins. It also runs themed events, so check the calendar if you have a specific night in mind.
Opening_Notes: Check the official event calendar and current entry conditions.

Name: HUNGR
Description: Community bar in Tallinn with drinks, music and a clearly queer social focus. Good for a lower-key start to the night or for meeting people outside the adult venues.
Opening_Notes: Check current social channels for opening hours and events.

Name: HELLo Bar
Description: Simple gay bar in Tallinn Old Town where you can have a drink without needing a big club plan. A sensible stop for visitors staying around the historic centre.
Opening_Notes: Check current social channels before visiting.

Name: Tallinn Bearty
Description: Annual bear-culture weekend and ongoing bear-community party brand in Tallinn. This is a searchable event parent rather than a day-to-day venue, so check the organiser for the current host space and tickets.
Opening_Notes: Check the official festival programme for the next dates, location and tickets.

Name: Tallinn Pride / Baltic Pride
Description: Tallinn’s major Pride-week listing, including Baltic Pride when Estonia hosts. The 2026 Baltic Pride programme brought a week of cultural, social and advocacy events to Tallinn; check the organisers for the next city and edition.
Opening_Notes: Baltic Pride rotates between Baltic capitals; check the official organiser for the next host city and dates.

Name: Riga Pride
Description: Annual Riga Pride week and city-centre march, with the main gathering and march programme typically centred on Esplanāde. Use this listing for the wider official programme rather than one specific bar party.
Opening_Notes: Citywide Pride programme. Check the official site for the next edition.

Name: By Queers For Queers
Native_Map_Query: By Queers For Queers, Aristida Briāna iela 9a, Riga, Latvia
Description: Riga’s current main queer club, reopened in 2026 after the earlier Skapis identity. Expect weekend parties, drag, DJs and a younger mixed LGBTQ+ crowd; check the current programme because the brand and schedule have recently changed.
Opening_Notes: Check current social channels for club nights and the latest schedule.

Name: TOP Club Riga
Description: LGBTQ+ nightclub with pop, electronic, drag and special-party programming. It is a good mainstream dance-floor option for a late weekend in Riga.
Opening_Notes: Check the current Facebook programme for opening nights and event details.

Name: Atbalss Gay Sauna
Native_Map_Query: Atbalss Gay Sauna, Alfrēda Kalniņa iela 8, Riga, Latvia
Description: Small central gay sauna with a compact, intimate setup. It is a practical adult-space option in Riga rather than a large spa-style sauna.
Opening_Notes: Check current opening hours directly before travelling.

Name: Bunker Cruising Bar
Native_Map_Query: Bunker Cruising Bar, Alfrēda Kalniņa iela 4, Riga, Latvia
Description: Gay cruise bar with darkrooms, video space, private rooms and regular underwear or leather-themed nights. The adult focus is clear, but mixed Saturday events can also appear on the calendar.
Opening_Notes: Check the current Instagram or club notice for daily opening and themed nights.

Name: Lithuanian Pride
Description: Lithuania’s main Pride festival in Vilnius, built around the March for Equality alongside cultural, community and concert programming. It is the city’s major annual LGBTQ+ visitor moment.
Opening_Notes: Citywide Pride programme. Check the official organiser for the next schedule and march route.

Name: Glamour Gay Sauna
Native_Map_Query: Glamour Gay Sauna, T. Ševčenkos g. 16i, Vilnius, Lithuania
Description: Vilnius’s dedicated gay sauna, with Finnish and steam sauna facilities, a whirlpool and weekend private party nights. A central adult-space option if you want more than the city’s club scene.
Opening_Notes: Weekend themed sessions change regularly; check the official party calendar.

Name: VOX Bar
Native_Map_Query: VOX Bar, Teatro g. 1, Vilnius, Lithuania
Description: LGBTQ+ bar in the centre with cocktails and a welcoming social atmosphere. Use it for a first drink or an easy meeting point before Soho.
Opening_Notes: Check current social channels for opening hours and events.

Name: SOHO Club
Native_Map_Query: SOHO Club, Švitrigailos g. 7, Vilnius, Lithuania
Description: Lithuania’s long-running LGBTQ+ nightclub, with a gay bar, two dancefloors, drag, DJs and party programming from pop to techno. This is the principal late-night queer club in Vilnius.
Vibe_Tags: Party, Dancefloor, Queer, Drag, Pop/Dance, Techno
Opening_Notes: Check the current schedule for drag, guest performers and event-specific entry.
